title: Zigadenus densus
taxonomy: Zigadenus densus is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and specifically belongs to the family Melanthiaceae. Within this family, it is classified as a genus (Zigadenus) and species (Z. densus). This classification places it in the order Liliales, which consists of flowering plants often referred to as lilies. The family Melanthiaceae is part of the monocot clade, which includes many flowering plants that have one cotyledon. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships between Zigadenus densus and other related plant species.

side_effects: Zigadenus densus, a plant commonly known as dense deathcamas, is toxic and can cause severe side effects if ingested. Symptoms of poisoning may include nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, which can progress to more serious conditions such as respiratory failure and cardiac arrest. Ingestion of the plant can also lead to neurological symptoms, including tremors, seizures, and coma. Treatment for Zigadenus densus poisoning typically involves supportive care, such as administering activated charcoal and fluid replacement.
